title: Carpacoce spermacocea
intro: Carpacoce spermacocea, also known as Spermacoce or False Buttonweed, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to support respiratory and digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Carpacoce spermacocea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
